evidence.rule_summary_from_author: When Range Crossing Behavior is zero, unlocked host reads/writes spanning multiple Locking ranges are processed. GenKey fills the targeted K_AES media-key object with new key material and Opal identifies GenKey band erase as cryptographic erase where the keys used to encrypt user data are changed. Therefore a host write spanning multiple effective Locking ranges must be tracked per range segment: a later GenKey on one segment's media key invalidates old data for that segment and any combined read containing it, while segments controlled by unchanged media keys remain readable.

CredentialObjectUID.GenKey [ PublicExponent = uinteger, PinLength = uinteger ] => [ ]
5.3.3.16.3.1 Result The GenKey method returns an empty list. Success or failure of the method invocation is determinable based on the status code returned in response to the method invocation.

5.7.2.3.4 Key This column stores the key associated with this K_AES_128 object. Non-tweakable cipher modes such as ECB, CBC, CFB, OFB, GCM, CCM, and CTR SHALL use the 16-byte option for the key size. Tweakable cipher modes such as XTS or LRW SHALL use the 32-byte option For MediaEncryption mode, the content of the Key column MAY be vendor-specific.

5.7.2.4.4 Key This column stores the key associated with this K_AES_256 object. Non-tweakable cipher modes such as ECB, CBC, CFB, OFB, GCM, CCM, and CTR SHALL use the 32-byte option for the key size. 4.3.7 Storage Device Read/Write Data Command Locking Behavior Interactions with Range Crossing If a Storage Device receives a read or write command that spans multiple Locking ranges and the Locking ranges are not locked, the Storage Device SHALL either: • Process the data transfer as defined in [2], if Range Crossing Behavior bit is set to zero (in Level 0 Discovery Opal SSC V2 Feature, see section 3.1.1.5) OR • Terminate the command with “Other Invalid Command Parameter” as defined in [4], if Range Crossing Behavior bit is set to one (in Level 0 Discovery Opal SSC V2 Feature, see section 3.1.1.5).

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: When Range Crossing Behavior is zero, unlocked host reads/writes spanning multiple Locking ranges are processed. GenKey fills the targeted K_AES media-key object with new key material and Opal identifies GenKey band erase as cryptographic erase where the keys used to encrypt user data are changed. Therefore a host write spanning multiple effective Locking ranges must be tracked per range segment: a later GenKey on one segment's media key invalidates old data for that segment and any combined read containing it, while segments controlled by unchanged media keys remain readable.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: When Range Crossing Behavior is zero, unlocked host reads/writes spanning multiple Locking ranges are processed. GenKey fills the targeted K_AES media-key object with new key material and Opal identifies GenKey band erase as cryptographic erase where the keys used to encrypt user data are changed. Therefore a host write spanning multiple effective Locking ranges must be tracked per range segment: a later GenKey on one segment's media key invalidates old data for that segment and any combined read containing it, while segments controlled by unchanged media keys remain readable.

case_name: GlobalRange GenKey leaves non-global segment readable

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: When Range Crossing Behavior is zero, unlocked host reads/writes spanning multiple Locking ranges are processed. GenKey fills the targeted K_AES media-key object with new key material and Opal identifies GenKey band erase as cryptographic erase where the keys used to encrypt user data are changed. Therefore a host write spanning multiple effective Locking ranges must be tracked per range segment: a later GenKey on one segment's media key invalidates old data for that segment and any combined read containing it, while segments controlled by unchanged media keys remain readable.

An existing object in any of the C_AES_*, K_AES_*, C_EC_*, C_PIN, C_RSA_* or C_HMAC_* tables is filled in with new key material This method fills in the new key as appropriate for the type of the credential on which the method was invoked.
